root_text.an10.65:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ āyasmā sāriputto magadhesu viharati nālakagāmake.
root_text.an10.65:1.2: Atha kho sāmaṇḍakāni paribbājako yenāyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā sāriputtena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an10.65:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho sāmaṇḍakāni paribbājako āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an10.65:2.1: “Kiṁ nu kho, āvuso sāriputta, sukhaṁ, kiṁ dukkhan”ti?
root_text.an10.65:2.3: Abhinibbattiyā, āvuso, sati idaṁ dukkhaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—
root_text.an10.65:2.4: sītaṁ uṇhaṁ jighacchā pipāsā uccāro passāvo aggisamphasso daṇḍasamphasso satthasamphasso ñātīpi mittāpi saṅgamma samāgamma rosenti.
root_text.an10.65:2.5: Abhinibbattiyā, āvuso, sati idaṁ dukkhaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ.
root_text.an10.65:2.6: Anabhinibbattiyā, āvuso, sati idaṁ sukhaṁ pāṭikaṅkhaṁ—
root_text.an10.65:2.7: na sītaṁ na uṇhaṁ na jighacchā na pipāsā na uccāro na passāvo na aggisamphasso na daṇḍasamphasso na satthasamphasso ñātīpi mittāpi saṅgamma samāgamma na rosenti.
root_text.an10.65:2.8: Anabhinibbattiyā, āvuso, sati idaṁ sukhaṁ pāṭikaṅkhan”ti.

translation_text.an10.65:1.1: At one time Venerable Sāriputta was staying in the land of the Magadhans near the little village of Nālaka.
translation_text.an10.65:1.2: Then the wanderer Sāmaṇḍakāni went up to Venerable Sāriputta and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an10.65:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, Sāmaṇḍakāni sat down to one side, and said to Sāriputta:
translation_text.an10.65:2.1: “Reverend Sāriputta, what is happiness and what is suffering?”
translation_text.an10.65:2.2: “Rebirth is suffering, reverend, no rebirth is happiness.
translation_text.an10.65:2.3: When there is rebirth, you can expect this kind of suffering.
translation_text.an10.65:2.4: Cold, heat, hunger, thirst, defecation, and urination. Contact with fire, clubs, and knives. And relatives and friends get together and annoy you.
translation_text.an10.65:2.5: When there is rebirth, this is the kind of suffering you can expect.
translation_text.an10.65:2.6: When there is no rebirth, you can expect this kind of happiness.
translation_text.an10.65:2.7: No cold, heat, hunger, thirst, defecation, or urination. No contact with fire, clubs, or knives. And relatives and friends don’t get together and annoy you.
translation_text.an10.65:2.8: When there is no rebirth, this is the kind of happiness you can expect.”
